title: Chemical Composition (Simplified Input)
description: A user-friendly form to describe the chemical composition of a material. Fill in the material name and list each element with its measured fraction. This input will be automatically converted to a standardised RDF representation (PMDCo OO-LD).

material_name.title: Material Name
material_name.description: A human-readable name or identifier for the material whose composition is being described. Example: '316L Stainless Steel', 'AlSi10Mg powder (batch A)'.

material_id.title: Material ID
material_id.description: Optional. Identifier for the Material node in the RDF graph. If omitted, derived automatically from material_name (e.g. '316L Stainless Steel' → 'mat-316l-stainless-steel'). The comp_id follows the same pattern with prefix 'chem-comp-'. Override only when you need a specific IRI to match an existing knowledge graph node.

comp_id.title: Composition ID
comp_id.description: Optional. Identifier for the ChemicalComposition node in the RDF graph. If omitted, derived automatically from material_name. Override only when you need a specific IRI.

elements.title: Element Fractions
elements.description: List of chemical elements present in the material and their measured fraction values. Add one entry per element. The fractions do not need to sum to exactly 100 (traces and balance elements are common), but each individual value must be between 0 and 100.

items.title: Element Entry
items.description: A single chemical element and its fraction in the composition.

symbol.title: Chemical Element
symbol.description: The symbol of the chemical element (e.g. 'Fe' for Iron, 'C' for Carbon). Only elements with a confirmed identity in the PMDCo ontology are accepted.
symbol.$comment: Full names: Hydrogen, Helium, Lithium, Beryllium, Boron, Carbon, Nitrogen, Oxygen, Fluorine, Neon, Sodium, Magnesium, Aluminium, Silicon, Phosphorus, Sulfur, Chlorine, Argon, Potassium, Calcium, Scandium, Titanium, Vanadium, Chromium, Manganese, Iron, Cobalt, Nickel, Copper, Zinc, Gallium, Germanium, Arsenic, Selenium, Bromine, Krypton, Yttrium, Zirconium, Niobium, Molybdenum, Ruthenium, Rhodium, Palladium, Silver, Cadmium, Indium, Tin, Antimony, Tellurium, Iodine, Xenon, Caesium, Barium, Lanthanum, Cerium, Neodymium, Samarium, Erbium, Hafnium, Tantalum, Tungsten, Rhenium, Osmium, Iridium, Platinum, Gold, Mercury, Thallium, Lead, Bismuth, Uranium, Bohrium

value.title: Fraction Value
value.description: The measured fraction of this element in the chosen unit. Must be a number between 0 (exclusive lower bound – use a very small positive number for traces) and 100 (maximum, 100% pure). Typical precision is 0.001.

unit.title: Fraction Unit
unit.description: The unit in which the fraction value is expressed. All elements in one composition should use the same unit. Choose one of: 'mass%' (mass percentage, most common in metallurgy), 'vol%' (volume percentage), 'mol%' (molar percentage).
